title: SystemVerilog + SystemC: Invalid operator& and lvalue required errors with PULP Platform AXI CDC design (v5.032)
body:
I am encountering C++ compilation errors when using Verilator with SystemC integration (-sc) on the PULP Platform AXI CDC module (from [pulp-platform/axi](https://github.com/pulp-platform/axi)
 and [pulp-platform/common_cells](https://github.com/pulp-platform/common_cells)
).
The generated C++ code fails to compile due to lvalue required and invalid operator& errors when building with SystemC.
This issue is reproducible with:
- SystemC 2.3.3
- SystemC 3.0.1
The same RTL compiles and runs correctly in other simulators.

**Environment:**
- Verilator version: v5.032
- SystemC version: 2.3.3.

**Steps to reproduce**
Files (from PULP axi and common_cells) placed in axi_cdc_example/:
- assign.svh
- axi_pkg.sv
- axi_cdc.sv
- axi_cdc_src.sv
- binary_to_gray.sv
- cdc_fifo_gray.sv
- gray_to_binary.sv
- my_cdc_wrapper.sv    <-- Top module
- registers.svh
- spill_register.sv
- spill_register_flushable.sv
- sync.sv
- typedef.svh

**The required files are attached:**
[axi_cdc_example.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21957943/axi_cdc_example.zip)

**Build commands::**
verilator -sc --timing \
  --top-module my_cdc_wrapper \
  -Wno-WIDTHEXPAND -Wno-WIDTHTRUNC \
  --Mdir my_cdc_dir --prefix Vmy_cdc_wrapper \
  axi_pkg.sv spill_register_flushable.sv spill_register.sv \
  sync.sv binary_to_gray.sv gray_to_binary.sv cdc_fifo_gray.sv \
  axi_cdc_src.sv axi_cdc.sv my_cdc_wrapper.sv

make -j4 VM_PARALLEL_BUILD=1 -C my_cdc_dir -f Vmy_cdc_wrapper.mk Vmy_cdc_wrapper__ALL.a

**Observed behavior**
The generated C++ fails to compile:
Vmy_cdc_wrapper___024root__DepSet_h2475c0fb__0.cpp: In function ‘void Vmy_cdc_wrapper___024root___nba_sequent__TOP__0(Vmy_cdc_wrapper___024root*)’:
Vmy_cdc_wrapper___024root__DepSet_h2475c0fb__0.cpp:1642:248: error: lvalue required as left operand of assignment
 1642 | _axi_cdc__DOT__i_axi_cdc_src__DOT__i_cdc_fifo_gray_dst_r__DOT__i_spill_register__DOT__spill_register_flushable_i__DOT__gen_spill_reg__DOT__b_data_q[0U];
      |                                                                                                                                                       ^

Vmy_cdc_wrapper___024root__DepSet_h2475c0fb__0.cpp:1720:49: error: invalid operands of types ‘unsigned int’ and ‘sc_core::sc_signal_inout_if<sc_dt::sc_bv<1094> >*’ to binary ‘operator&’
 1720 |     vlSelfRef.slave_axi_resp_o[0x22U] = ((0x3cU & vlSelfRef.slave_axi_resp_o[0x22U])
      |                                           ~~~~~ ^ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      |                                           |                                       |
      |                                           unsigned int                            sc_core::sc_signal_inout_if<sc_dt::sc_bv<1094> >*

**Additional Observation:**
- The errors disappear if the signal `mem_resp_s  slave_axi_resp_o` is not declared as an output port of my_cdc_wrapper but instead as an internal signal within the module. 

**Notes:**
- This suggests that the problem may be specifically related to Verilator’s handling of struct-typed output ports in SystemC integration mode (-sc).
- The design uses typedef.svh and axi_pkg.sv from PULP’s common infrastructure and works fine in other simulators.
Problem seems specific to -sc (SystemC integration mode).

**Questions:**
- Is this a known issue in Verilator’s C++ code generation for SystemC?
- Are there any recommended workarounds?
